description:
You are working as a localization engineer on a legacy code-base located under “/home/user/project/”.  
Inside “/home/user/project/translations/” there is a single German resource file named  

    /home/user/project/translations/messages_de.properties  

Currently this file is saved in the **ISO-8859-1** character set.  
Your team has decided that *all* resource files must be in **UTF-8** from now on, and, for traceability, every conversion must leave a short audit trail.

Your assignment is to:

1. Convert “messages_de.properties” from ISO-8859-1 to UTF-8 and save the converted copy as  

       /home/user/project/translations/messages_de_utf8.properties

2. Pre-pend the following single comment line **exactly as shown** (no leading or trailing spaces) at the very top of the converted file:  

       # Converted to UTF-8 on 2023-12-01

   The original key-value pairs must appear **unaltered and on their own separate lines** immediately after this header.

3. Create a conversion log called  

       /home/user/project/conversion.log  

   Append **one** new record to it, following this comma-separated format (CSV, no spaces):  

       source_file,target_file,old_encoding,new_encoding,conversion_date

   For this task, the exact record that **must** be written is:  

       translations/messages_de.properties,translations/messages_de_utf8.properties,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8,2023-12-01

   Notes on the log:
   • The file must end with a single newline character.  
   • If “conversion.log” already exists, add the new line **after** whatever is there; do not delete prior data.

Verification notes the automated test will perform:
• It will check that “/home/user/project/translations/messages_de_utf8.properties” exists, is encoded in UTF-8, and that its very first line is the exact header shown above.  
• It will confirm that the two expected key-value pairs are present and byte-for-byte identical to the originals.  
• It will inspect “/home/user/project/conversion.log” and confirm that it contains the exact CSV record shown above, ending with a newline and without extra spaces.

Make sure the line endings remain UNIX style (LF only).  
You do **not** need root privileges, and only standard command-line utilities available on a minimal Debian/Ubuntu image may be used.  
When you are finished, the system should precisely match the state described above.
truth:
Initial environment (provided before the agent runs):

1. The directory “/home/user/project/translations/” exists (0755).
2. The file “/home/user/project/translations/messages_de.properties” exists (0644) and is encoded in ISO-8859-1 with the following three lines and a trailing LF:

   greeting=Grüß Gott
   farewell=Auf Wiedersehen
   thanks=Vielen Dank

   (The bytes for “ü” and “ß” are 0xFC and 0xDF respectively in ISO-8859-1.)
3. “/home/user/project/conversion.log” either does **not** exist or is an empty regular file.

Expected state **after** successful task completion:

1. “/home/user/project/translations/messages_de_utf8.properties” exists, permissions 0644.
   • It is valid UTF-8 (tested with “iconv -f UTF-8 -t UTF-8”).  
   • Its exact byte sequence (showed here in UTF-8, each line ending in LF) is:

     # Converted to UTF-8 on 2023-12-01⏎
     greeting=Grüß Gott⏎
     farewell=Auf Wiedersehen⏎
     thanks=Vielen Dank⏎

2. “/home/user/project/conversion.log” exists, permissions 0644, and ends with a single LF.
   • It contains the line:

     translations/messages_de.properties,translations/messages_de_utf8.properties,ISO-8859-1,UTF-8,2023-12-01⏎

   • If any previous lines were present they must remain above this line.

No other files or directories are to be created or modified.
